---
title: "Wildfire surges in East, Southeast US fueled by new trees and shrubs"
date: "2024-12-17"
description: "Woody vegetation has increased by 37% over the last 30 years in the eastern United States, fueling the rise in large wildfires. Texas and the Appalachian Mountains took the biggest hits."
categories: ["Media Coverage"]
pub-authors: "Lester, L."
pub-venue: "AGU News"
pub-url: "https://news.agu.org/press-release/shrub-growth-fuels-eastern-wildfires/"
---

::: {.nw-cite-meta}
Lester, L. (2024). *AGU News*.
:::

[Source](https://news.agu.org/press-release/shrub-growth-fuels-eastern-wildfires/){.nw-btn .nw-btn-primary target="_blank"} [BibTeX](cite.bib){.nw-btn .nw-btn-ghost}

## Abstract

Woody vegetation has increased by 37% over the last 30 years in the eastern United States, fueling the rise in large wildfires. Texas and the Appalachian Mountains took the biggest hits.
